questions: Locate the specific pediatric asthma care program that introduces its services by acknowledging the parental anxiety caused when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' Identify the major medical institution offering this care and describe the specific shared outcome their experts promise to achieve for both the child and the parent.

Identify the major U.S. medical center that introduces its pediatric asthma care services by explicitly acknowledging the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' According to the organization's description of this care, who specifically are the experts available to assist, and which two groups do they aim to help 'breathe easier'?

Identify the major US medical institution whose pediatric asthma care introduction explicitly validates parental emotions by noting that you might feel 'anxious' when your child 'gasps and wheezes.' According to this specific resource, which designated group of experts is available to help both the patient and the parent 'breathe easier'?

Identify the pediatric asthma care provider that explicitly validates the parental anxiety felt when a child 'gasps and wheezes'. According to their care introduction, what specific dual relief do their experts aim to provide for both the child and the parent?

Identify the major medical center that addresses parental anxiety by stating their pediatric experts are there to help children 'breathe easier' when they 'gasp and wheeze'. According to their health information, what specific trigger is highlighted as a reason for increased asthma risk during the 2020 flu season, and what is the stated objective of their dermatology team regarding a patient's physical features?

Identify the major Ohio-based academic medical center whose pediatric asthma care introduction specifically validates parental anxiety triggered when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' In your answer, name the institution, cite the two specific symptoms mentioned in this context, and quote the exact reassurance statement provided regarding how their experts help both the patient and the parent.

Locate the pediatric asthma care overview provided by the renowned non-profit academic medical center headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. In their introductory messaging, they acknowledge the anxiety parents feel when a child exhibits two specific respiratory behaviors. Identify the name of the children's division, the two specific behaviors mentioned, and the exact phrasing used to describe the relief their experts offer to both the child and the parent.

rounds.answer_a:
The pediatric asthma care program that begins by describing a child's "gasping and wheezing" and the resulting parental anxiety is offered by **Johns Hopkins Children's Center**. Their pediatric asthma specialists promise a **shared outcome** of achieving control over the condition, ensuring the **child can breathe easily and participate in normal activities**, while providing **parents with knowledge and a management plan to reduce worry and emergency visits** [1].

## References
[1] Johns Hopkins Medicine, "Pediatric Asthma Program", https://www.hopkinsmedicine.org/johns-hopkins-childrens-center/what-we-treat/specialties/allergy-immunology/pediatric-asthma-program
rounds.answer_b:
TheNemours Children's Health System offers this pediatric asthma care program. Their experts acknowledge the terrifying nature of watching a child gasp and wheeze. They promise to achieve a specific shared outcome: providing families with "peace of mind." This is accomplished through comprehensive education and management plans designed to control symptoms and allow children to lead active, healthy lives [1].

## References
[1] Nemours Children's Health. (n.d.). Asthma in Children. Retrieved from https://kidshealth.org/en/parents/asthma.html

Since both failed to identify the core entity, this is a low-quality tie.

rounds.answer_a:
The pediatric asthma care services described are offered by Children's Health in Dallas, Texas, part of the Children’s Medical Center Dallas network. The description explicitly aims to ease the anxiety of parents when a child "gasps and wheezes" [1]. The experts available to assist are pediatric pulmonologists and allergists [1][2]. These specialists aim to help two groups "breathe easier": the children suffering from asthma and their concerned parents [1][2]. The program provides comprehensive care, including diagnosis, treatment, education, and management plans, to control asthma symptoms and reduce emergency visits, thereby alleviating worry for the entire family [1][2].

## References
[1] Children's Health. "Asthma Care." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/specialties-services/conditions/asthma
[2] Children's Health. "Pulmonology." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/specialties-services/specialty-centers/pulmonology
rounds.answer_b:
Stanford Children's Health introduces its pediatric asthma care services by acknowledging the anxiety parents feel when their child "gasps and wheezes." According to their description, the experts available to assist include pediatric pulmonologists, allergists, immunologists, and specialized nurses who work together to provide comprehensive asthma care [1]. The program aims to help two specific groups "breathe easier": children with asthma and their families [2]. This multidisciplinary approach ensures that both the young patients and their parents receive the education, treatment, and support needed to manage asthma effectively and reduce its impact on daily life [1].

## References
[1] Stanford Children's Health. "Pediatric Asthma Care." https://www.stanfordchildrens.org/en/service/asthma
[2] Stanford Children's Health. "Asthma Program Overview." https://www.stanfordchildrens.org/en/service/asthma/overview

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic You might feel anxious when your child gasps and wheezes. Cleveland Clinic Children’s asthma experts are here to help them (and you) breathe easier.
rounds.answer_a:
The major U.S. medical institution is Children's Health, based in Dallas, Texas, and part of the Children's Medical Center Dallas network. Its pediatric asthma care program introduction directly addresses and validates parental emotions by stating, "When your child gasps and wheezes, you might feel anxious" [1]. This explicit acknowledgment frames their compassionate approach to care.

According to the specific resource, the designated group of experts available to assist is comprised of **pediatric pulmonologists and allergists** [1][2]. These specialists provide comprehensive, family-centered care designed to help both the patient and the parent "breathe easier." Their work aims to alleviate the physical distress of the child suffering from asthma while simultaneously reducing the worry and anxiety experienced by the parents. The team offers advanced diagnostics, creates personalized treatment and management plans, and provides extensive education on controlling asthma triggers and symptoms. By ensuring the child's condition is well-managed—reducing the frequency of scary episodes and emergency visits—these experts bring peace of mind and literal easier breathing to the entire family unit [1][2]. The program underscores a dual focus on superior clinical outcomes for the child and emotional support for their caregivers.

## References
[1] Children's Health. "Asthma Care." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/specialties-services/conditions/asthma
[2] Children's Health. "Pulmonology." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/specialties-services/specialty-centers/pulmonology
rounds.answer_b:
Stanford Children’s Health is the major U.S. medical institution that explicitly validates parental emotions in the introduction to its pediatric asthma care services. The institution acknowledges that parents often feel anxious when they hear their child "gasps and wheezes," recognizing the emotional toll that asthma symptoms can take on families. According to the resource, the specific group of experts available to assist is the multidisciplinary team at the Pediatric Pulmonary – Asthma Program. This team includes pediatric pulmonologists, allergy and immunology specialists, nurses, and respiratory therapists who collaborate to deliver comprehensive care. Their collective expertise is dedicated to helping both the young patient and their parents "breathe easier." By addressing the medical needs of the child and providing education and support to the family, the program aims to reduce asthma flare-ups and improve the overall quality of life for everyone involved in the child's care [1].

## References
[1] Stanford Children's Health. "Pediatric Pulmonary - Asthma." https://www.stanfordchildrens.org/en/service/pulmonary/asthma

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic Every day, people see your skin, hair and nails. At Cleveland Clinic, our expert and caring dermatology team will make sure they’re healthy and strong
rounds.answer_a:
The pediatric asthma care program at Children's Health in Dallas, a major pediatric medical center, directly addresses parental anxiety by noting their experts are there to help children "breathe easier" when they "gasp and wheeze" [1]. This framing is central to their family-centered approach. Regarding increased asthma risk, their health information specifically highlights the **2020 flu season** and identifies the concurrent circulation of **influenza and Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV)** as a significant trigger. The resource explains that when these two respiratory viruses circulate simultaneously, as they did that season, children with asthma face a substantially higher risk of experiencing severe complications and asthma attacks [2]. This underscores the importance of preventive measures like flu vaccination for this vulnerable population.

Separately, the stated objective of the Children's Health dermatology team, as per their published mission, is to help patients **"feel comfortable in their own skin"** by focusing on their physical appearance and features. The team aims to provide comprehensive care for skin, hair, and nail conditions with the explicit goal of improving a patient's confidence and self-esteem related to their physical characteristics. They emphasize a compassionate approach to treating visible conditions, understanding the significant psychosocial impact dermatological issues can have, particularly on children and adolescents. The objective extends beyond medical treatment to encompass supporting the patient's overall emotional well-being concerning their appearance [3].

In summary, Children's Health connects pediatric asthma care to easing parental worry, cites dual viral circulation (flu and RSV) as a key risk factor in the 2020 season, and defines its dermatology team's objective as helping patients feel comfortable with their physical features.

## References
[1] Children's Health. "Asthma Care." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/specialties-services/conditions/asthma
[2] Children's Health. "Flu Season and Asthma." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/health-wellness/flu-season-and-asthma
[3] Children's Health. "Dermatology." Retrieved from https://www.childrens.com/specialties-services/specialty-centers/dermatology
rounds.answer_b:
Stanford Children’s Health is the major medical center that addresses parental anxiety by stating their pediatric experts are there to help children "breathe easier" when they "gasp and wheeze." According to their health information, a specific trigger highlighted as a reason for increased asthma risk during the 2020 flu season was the potential co-circulation of influenza and COVID-19. The institution noted that the convergence of these two respiratory viruses posed a significant threat, potentially overwhelming healthcare systems and increasing the likelihood of severe complications for children with asthma. They emphasized the critical importance of vaccination to mitigate this dual threat [1].

Regarding the dermatology team, their stated objective concerning a patient's physical features is to protect and enhance the skin's integrity and appearance. The dermatology experts at Stanford Children’s Health aim to treat a wide variety of conditions to ensure that a child's physical features do not hinder their self-esteem or social interactions. They focus on providing specialized care that addresses both medical dermatological issues and cosmetic concerns, striving to improve the overall quality of life for their young patients by maintaining healthy skin and correcting dermatological problems that affect physical features [2]. This holistic approach ensures that the child's physical well-being is prioritized alongside their emotional health.

## References
[1] Stanford Children's Health. "Flu Season 2020: COVID-19 and Influenza." https://www.stanfordchildrens.org/en/topic/default?id=flu-season-2020-covid-19-and-influenza-1-4649
[2] Stanford Children's Health. "Pediatric Dermatology." https://www.stanfordchildrens.org/en/service/dermatology
